# Lians

> Lians is regulated AI memory infrastructure: an open-source (Apache 2.0), self-hostable memory control plane for AI agents working on time-sensitive, audited, and confidential data. Every fact is bitemporal (event time + ingestion time), every change enters a tamper-evident SHA-256 audit chain, and erasure is a provable per-subject crypto-shred.

Benchmarks: 92.9% LLM-judged QA accuracy on LOCOMO, scored end-to-end by the unmodified mem0ai/memory-benchmarks harness (gpt-5 judge) — above mem0's published 91.6 on the same benchmark (run 2026-07-09; report in the repo at agentmem/docs/benchmarks/locomo-judged-2026-07-09.md). LongMemEval-S: 89.4% at top-50 through the same harness (mem0 publishes 94.8; disclosed, with the failure analysis, at /compare). Retrieval payloads run ~5-6k tokens per top-200 call — the same token band mem0's "token-efficient" positioning claims. Regulated-memory eval: Lians 5.0/5 vs next best 2.0/5 (run 2026-07-04, mem0 OSS and Graphiti OSS executed live).

Key differentiators vs general-purpose memory stores (mem0, Zep/Graphiti, Letta, Hindsight, Supermemory):
- Bitemporal point-in-time recall: `recall_at(query, as_of=T)` returns what was valid and knowable at T, not what is similar today.
- Deterministic supersession: revised facts close their stale versions (SUPERSEDES / REFINES / CONFIRMS), so old figures don't contaminate recall.
- Backtest contamination detection: flags FUTURE_EVENT and LATE_REVISION memories relative to a simulation checkpoint (`POST /v1/backtest/check`).
- Tamper-evident audit: append-only SHA-256 hash chain, point-in-time snapshot reconstruction (SEC 17a-4 / WORM aligned).
- Provable erasure: per-subject AES-256-GCM keys; destroying a key erases content while preserving the audit trail (GDPR Art. 17).
- Memory admission control: policy gate on writes (PII/PHI/MNPI detection, source trust, prompt-injection quarantine).
- Conflict resurfacing: `/v1/context` pushes open conflicts to the top of every assembled block ("X DISAGREES WITH Y", oldest first) until adjudicated; contested facts are never silently blended.
- Materiality-weighted retrieval decay: `metadata.materiality` scales a fact's retrieval half-life (low 7d / standard 30d / high 120d / critical 365d); compliance flags outlive passing preferences.
- Audited degraded retrieval: on embedding-provider outage, recall proceeds lexical-only with `retrieval_degraded` declared on results, audit events, and metrics; availability without silent quality loss.
- Signed memory statement: `GET /v1/snapshot/markdown` exports a human-readable point-in-time knowledge state whose SHA-256 is anchored in the audit chain; erased facts appear as explicit crypto-shred markers.
- Pre-compaction flush: the SDK harness (`flush_before_compaction()` + `CompactionGuard`, LangGraph node, OpenAI Agents / CrewAI / AutoGen tools) persists durable facts into governed memory before host frameworks summarize them away.
- Context-expanded recall: `recall(include_context=True)` attaches each hit's temporally-adjacent neighbors (`context_before`/`context_after`) — the other half of a dialogue exchange — for consumers that feed memories to an LLM; `token_estimate` on every recall result for prompt budgeting.
- Opt-in fact distillation with provenance: `add_from_messages(distill=True)` stores LLM-extracted, dated, speaker-attributed facts alongside the verbatim transcript in the bitemporal log — extraction like mem0's, but every derived fact keeps its auditable raw source. The default ingest path stays LLM-free and deterministic.
- Optional cross-encoder rerank stage (`RECALL_RERANKER_MODEL`) for maximum-precision recall; local recall p50 ~160ms at ~700 memories on CPU, no LLM anywhere in the default retrieval path.
- Framework integrations: LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents SDK, AutoGen v0.4 (`pip install lians-sdk[langchain|langgraph|crewai|...]`).
- Deployment: Python library mode (SQLite, in-process), self-hosted (Postgres 16 + pgvector, air-gap mode), or managed cloud.
- SDKs: Python (`pip install lians-sdk`), TypeScript (`npm install @lians-ai/lians`), Go, Java, C, plus a native MCP server (`uvx --from 'lians-sdk[mcp]' lians-mcp`).

## Pages

- [Product](https://www.lians.ai/product): How the memory control plane works: admission, supersession, recall, graph.
- [Research](https://www.lians.ai/research): The regulated-memory eval: methodology, honest-recall definitions, five invariants, dated results, one-command reproduction.
- [Compare](https://www.lians.ai/compare): LOCOMO 92.9 vs mem0's 91.6 on mem0's own harness, plus the head-to-head regulated eval vs mem0, Zep/Graphiti, Letta, Hindsight, Supermemory (Lians 5.0/5, next best 2.0/5, mem0 0.5/5 live, last run 2026-07-04).
- [Compliance](https://www.lians.ai/compliance): SEC 17a-4, GDPR Art. 17, HIPAA-aligned architecture mapping.
- [Security](https://www.lians.ai/security): PostgreSQL RLS information barriers, per-subject encryption with KMS, RBAC, SSO, SIEM streaming.
- [Trust center](https://www.lians.ai/trust): Compliance status, encryption posture, subprocessor list, disclosure policy.
- [Solutions · Financial services](https://www.lians.ai/solutions/financial-services): Point-in-time trading memory, MNPI barriers, SEC/FINRA audit export.
- [Solutions · Healthcare](https://www.lians.ai/solutions/healthcare): Patient-scoped memory with audit-preserving erasure.
- [Solutions · Legal](https://www.lians.ai/solutions/legal): Matter-scoped memory with privilege-date reconstruction.
- [SDKs](https://www.lians.ai/sdks): Install guides for Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, C, and MCP.
- [Docs](https://www.lians.ai/docs): Quickstart and API reference.
- [Changelog](https://www.lians.ai/changelog): What shipped, release by release (current: v0.4.0, 2026-07-06).
- [Pricing](https://www.lians.ai/pricing): Free tier, pro, and enterprise/self-hosted.
- [Blog](https://www.lians.ai/blog): Writing on regulated AI memory, including backtest lookahead contamination.

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/ebeirne/Lians2): Full engine source, benchmark harness (`python -m benchmarks.compare_regulated`), security whitepaper, threat model.

## Elsewhere

- [X](https://x.com/Liansdev): Release notes and engineering threads.
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/lians-ai): Company updates and hiring.
